South Africa COVID19 Data Analysis

Mufaro Chinake

21/04/2022

1.Introduction

www.corona-stats.co.za launched a free API for historical South African COVID-19 data in April 2020. Data can be accessed using the link:"http://corona-stats.mobi/api/. This project entails accessing South African COVID-19 data from the API, compiling accessed data into useable datasets,Exploratory data analysis,data cleaning and representing the data in useable format i.e. data visualization utilizing Tableau.

2.Data access

To extract data from the API, R programming language packages:httr and Jsonlite were used. Some chunks of code are written as comments because when the notebook was complied the API key had expired. 

#library(httr)
#library(jsonlite)
#library(dplyr)

#base_url = "http://corona-stats.mobi/api/"
#info_url = "json.2.0.php?key=b1fQnVw3MoTAyqNmkigJ"
#full_url = base::paste0(base_url,info_url)

#api_call = httr::GET(full_url)
#knitr::opts_chunk$set(error = TRUE)

To check whether the request was successful the status code has to be 200. If it is around 400 it means the request was unsuccessful. 

#api_call$status_code

Converting the response to usable format 

#api_char = base::rawToChar(api_call$content)

#str(api_char)

#api_Json = jsonlite::fromJSON(api_char,flatten = T)

#api_Json
#str(api_Json)
#Covid19_RSA = api_Json$RSA

The Covid19 data is in the RSA list.RSA contains 22 lists of South Africa Covid19 data.

3.Compiling accessed data to useable datasets

  1. National Data 

#Dates1 = Covid19_RSA$Dates
#View(Dates1)

#Recoveries = SA_data$Recoveries

#Daily_Vaccinations = SA_data$`Daily Vaccinations`

#Vaccinations_14day_average = SA_data$`Vaccinations 14 day average`

  1. Exploratory Data Analysis

All the provincial dataframes were combined into 1 dataset, Sa_ProvincialCOVID19. 

library(readxl)
## Warning: package 'readxl' was built under R version 4.1.3
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
WC = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/WesternCapeCovid19.xlsx")
WC_data = mutate(WC,Province = "Western Cape")
Gauteng = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/GautengCovid19.xlsx")
Gauteng_data = mutate(Gauteng,Province = "Gauteng")
KZN = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/KZNCovid19.xlsx")
KZN_data = mutate(KZN,Province = "KwaZulu-Natal")
EC = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/ECCovid19.xlsx")
EC_data = mutate(EC,Province = "Eastern Cape")
FS = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/FSCovid19.xlsx")
FS_data = mutate(FS,Province = "Free State")
MP = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/MPCovid19.xlsx")
MP_data = mutate(MP,Province = "Mpumalanga")
LP = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/LPCovid19.xlsx")
LP_data = mutate(LP, Province = "Limpopo")
NW = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/NWCovid19.xlsx")
NW_data = mutate(NW,Province = "North West")
NC = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/NCCovid19.xlsx")
NC_data = mutate(NC,Province = "Northern Cape")

Sa_ProvincialCOVID19 = rbind(WC_data,KZN_data,Gauteng_data,EC_data,FS_data,MP_data,LP_data,NW_data,NC_data)
Sa_ProvincialCOVID19 = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/Sa_ProvincialCOVID19.xlsx")
mydata = Sa_ProvincialCOVID19

#COVID19 National data
data = read_excel("~/GitHub/South-Africa-COVID19/SaCovid19.xlsx")

A. Basic data profiling

The number of rowns and number of columns of Provincial COVID19 dataset 

nrow(mydata)
## [1] 7011
ncol(mydata)
## [1] 9

The number of rows and columns of the COVID19 National dataset: 

nrow(data)
## [1] 779
ncol(data)
## [1] 12

The data types of the COVID19 provincial data 

library(funModeling)
## Warning: package 'funModeling' was built under R version 4.1.3
## Loading required package: Hmisc
## Warning: package 'Hmisc' was built under R version 4.1.3
## Loading required package: lattice
## Warning: package 'lattice' was built under R version 4.1.3
## Loading required package: survival
## Warning: package 'survival' was built under R version 4.1.3
## Loading required package: Formula
## Loading required package: ggplot2
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.1.3
## 
## Attaching package: 'Hmisc'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     src, summarize
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     format.pval, units
## funModeling v.1.9.4 :)
## Examples and tutorials at livebook.datascienceheroes.com
##  / Now in Spanish: librovivodecienciadedatos.ai
df_status(mydata)
##               variable q_zeros p_zeros q_na p_na q_inf p_inf           type
## 1                 Date       0    0.00    0    0     0     0 POSIXct/POSIXt
## 2          Total_Cases     130    1.85    0    0     0     0      character
## 3     Total_Recoveries     441    6.29    0    0     0     0      character
## 4         Total_Deaths     539    7.69    0    0     0     0      character
## 5          Daily_Cases     261    3.72    0    0     0     0      character
## 6         Daily_Deaths    2989   42.63    0    0     0     0      character
## 7  Cases_14day_average     215    3.07    0    0     0     0      character
## 8 Deaths_14day_average    1048   14.95    0    0     0     0      character
## 9             Province       0    0.00    0    0     0     0      character
##   unique
## 1    779
## 2   6482
## 3   5034
## 4   3569
## 5   1681
## 6    209
## 7   1677
## 8    155
## 9      9

Data types for the COVID19 National dataset 

df_status(data)
##                      variable q_zeros p_zeros q_na p_na q_inf p_inf
## 1                        Date       0    0.00    0    0     0     0
## 2                 Total_Cases       3    0.39    0    0     0     0
## 3            Total_Recoveries      25    3.21    0    0     0     0
## 4                Total_Deaths      25    3.21    0    0     0     0
## 5                 Total_Tests       8    1.03    0    0     0     0
## 6          Total_Vaccinations     422   54.17    0    0     0     0
## 7                 Daily_Cases       4    0.51    0    0     0     0
## 8                Daily_Deaths      37    4.75    0    0     0     0
## 9          Daily_Vaccinations     429   55.07    0    0     0     0
## 10        Cases_14day_average      13    1.67    0    0     0     0
## 11       Deaths_14day_average      48    6.16    0    0     0     0
## 12 Vaccinations_14day_average     423   54.30    0    0     0     0
##              type unique
## 1  POSIXct/POSIXt    779
## 2       character    776
## 3       character    717
## 4       character    743
## 5       character    771
## 6       character    352
## 7       character    736
## 8       character    308
## 9       character    350
## 10      character    710
## 11      character    299
## 12      character    353

Columns q_zeros and p_zeros show the number of zeros and percentage of zeros per column respectively. Columns q_na and p_na show the number of na values and percentage of na values respectively.Column type shows the data types for all the columns. Some numerical values are read as characters above and also the date format needs to be changed. 

mydata$Date = as.Date(mydata$Date)
mydata$Daily_Deaths = as.numeric(mydata$Daily_Deaths)
mydata$Total_Cases = as.numeric(mydata$Total_Cases)
mydata$Total_Recoveries = as.numeric(mydata$Total_Recoveries)
mydata$Total_Deaths = as.numeric(mydata$Total_Deaths)
mydata$Daily_Cases = as.numeric(mydata$Daily_Cases)
mydata$Cases_14day_average = as.numeric(mydata$Cases_14day_average)
mydata$Deaths_14day_average = as.numeric(mydata$Deaths_14day_average)

df_status(mydata)
##               variable q_zeros p_zeros q_na p_na q_inf p_inf      type unique
## 1                 Date       0    0.00    0    0     0     0      Date    779
## 2          Total_Cases     130    1.85    0    0     0     0   numeric   6482
## 3     Total_Recoveries     441    6.29    0    0     0     0   numeric   5034
## 4         Total_Deaths     539    7.69    0    0     0     0   numeric   3569
## 5          Daily_Cases     261    3.72    0    0     0     0   numeric   1681
## 6         Daily_Deaths    2989   42.63    0    0     0     0   numeric    209
## 7  Cases_14day_average     215    3.07    0    0     0     0   numeric   1677
## 8 Deaths_14day_average    1048   14.95    0    0     0     0   numeric    155
## 9             Province       0    0.00    0    0     0     0 character      9
data$Date = as.Date(data$Date)
data$Daily_Deaths = as.numeric(data$Daily_Deaths)
data$Total_Cases = as.numeric(data$Total_Cases)
data$Total_Recoveries = as.numeric(data$Total_Recoveries)
data$Total_Deaths = as.numeric(data$Total_Deaths)
data$Daily_Cases = as.numeric(data$Daily_Cases)
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
data$Cases_14day_average = as.numeric(data$Cases_14day_average)
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
data$Deaths_14day_average = as.numeric(data$Deaths_14day_average)
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
data$Total_Tests = as.numeric(data$Total_Tests)
data$Total_Vaccinations = as.numeric(data$Total_Vaccinations)
data$Vaccinations_14day_average = as.numeric(data$Vaccinations_14day_average)
## Warning: NAs introduced by coercion
data$Daily_Vaccinations = as.numeric(data$Daily_Vaccinations)

df_status(data)
##                      variable q_zeros p_zeros q_na p_na q_inf p_inf    type
## 1                        Date       0    0.00    0 0.00     0     0    Date
## 2                 Total_Cases       3    0.39    0 0.00     0     0 numeric
## 3            Total_Recoveries      25    3.21    0 0.00     0     0 numeric
## 4                Total_Deaths      25    3.21    0 0.00     0     0 numeric
## 5                 Total_Tests       8    1.03    0 0.00     0     0 numeric
## 6          Total_Vaccinations     422   54.17    0 0.00     0     0 numeric
## 7                 Daily_Cases       4    0.51    2 0.26     0     0 numeric
## 8                Daily_Deaths      37    4.75    0 0.00     0     0 numeric
## 9          Daily_Vaccinations     429   55.07    0 0.00     0     0 numeric
## 10        Cases_14day_average      13    1.67   15 1.93     0     0 numeric
## 11       Deaths_14day_average      48    6.16    2 0.26     0     0 numeric
## 12 Vaccinations_14day_average     423   54.30    2 0.26     0     0 numeric
##    unique
## 1     779
## 2     776
## 3     717
## 4     743
## 5     771
## 6     352
## 7     735
## 8     308
## 9     350
## 10    709
## 11    298
## 12    352

All the column data types have been corrected. To find the statistical properties of the data: 

summary(mydata)
##       Date             Total_Cases      Total_Recoveries   Total_Deaths  
##  Min.   :2020-03-02   Min.   :      0   Min.   :      0   Min.   :    0  
##  1st Qu.:2020-09-12   1st Qu.:  30644   1st Qu.:  25607   1st Qu.:  526  
##  Median :2021-03-26   Median : 108929   Median :  98615   Median : 2896  
##  Mean   :2021-03-26   Mean   : 187694   Mean   : 174646   Mean   : 5417  
##  3rd Qu.:2021-10-07   3rd Qu.: 225022   3rd Qu.: 198932   3rd Qu.: 9503  
##  Max.   :2022-04-19   Max.   :1220511   Max.   :1195367   Max.   :21916  
##   Daily_Cases     Daily_Deaths     Cases_14day_average Deaths_14day_average
##  Min.   :-6624   Min.   :-186.00   Min.   :    0.0     Min.   : -4.0       
##  1st Qu.:   49   1st Qu.:   0.00   1st Qu.:   59.0     1st Qu.:  2.0       
##  Median :  171   Median :   2.00   Median :  192.0     Median :  5.0       
##  Mean   :  534   Mean   :  14.29   Mean   :  532.8     Mean   : 14.3       
##  3rd Qu.:  502   3rd Qu.:  15.00   3rd Qu.:  506.5     3rd Qu.: 16.0       
##  Max.   :17716   Max.   : 515.00   Max.   :10940.0     Max.   :164.0       
##    Province        
##  Length:7011       
##  Class :character  
##  Mode  :character  
##                    
##                    
##

There appears to be an error in the Daily_Deaths,Daily_Cases and Deaths_14day_average columns. The minimum values are negative and that needs to be rectified. All the negative numbers will be replaced by the variable’s absolute value. 

mydata_sorted = mydata[order(mydata$Date),]
mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases = abs(mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases)
mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths = abs(mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths)
mydata_sorted$Deaths_14day_average = abs(mydata_sorted$Deaths_14day_average)
summary(mydata_sorted)
##       Date             Total_Cases      Total_Recoveries   Total_Deaths  
##  Min.   :2020-03-02   Min.   :      0   Min.   :      0   Min.   :    0  
##  1st Qu.:2020-09-12   1st Qu.:  30644   1st Qu.:  25607   1st Qu.:  526  
##  Median :2021-03-26   Median : 108929   Median :  98615   Median : 2896  
##  Mean   :2021-03-26   Mean   : 187694   Mean   : 174646   Mean   : 5417  
##  3rd Qu.:2021-10-07   3rd Qu.: 225022   3rd Qu.: 198932   3rd Qu.: 9503  
##  Max.   :2022-04-19   Max.   :1220511   Max.   :1195367   Max.   :21916  
##   Daily_Cases       Daily_Deaths    Cases_14day_average Deaths_14day_average
##  Min.   :    0.0   Min.   :  0.00   Min.   :    0.0     Min.   :  0.00      
##  1st Qu.:   49.0   1st Qu.:  0.00   1st Qu.:   59.0     1st Qu.:  2.00      
##  Median :  172.0   Median :  2.00   Median :  192.0     Median :  5.00      
##  Mean   :  536.2   Mean   : 14.41   Mean   :  532.8     Mean   : 14.31      
##  3rd Qu.:  503.0   3rd Qu.: 15.00   3rd Qu.:  506.5     3rd Qu.: 16.00      
##  Max.   :17716.0   Max.   :515.00   Max.   :10940.0     Max.   :164.00      
##    Province        
##  Length:7011       
##  Class :character  
##  Mode  :character  
##                    
##                    
## 
summary(data)
##       Date             Total_Cases      Total_Recoveries   Total_Deaths   
##  Min.   :2020-03-02   Min.   :      0   Min.   :      0   Min.   :     0  
##  1st Qu.:2020-09-12   1st Qu.: 649004   1st Qu.: 577164   1st Qu.: 15437

##  1st Qu.: 3910632   1st Qu.:       0   1st Qu.: 1101   1st Qu.: 29.0

##  Max.   :24212994   Max.   :34366442   Max.   :37875   Max.   :844.0  
##                                        NA's   :2                      
##  Daily_Vaccinations Cases_14day_average Deaths_14day_average
##  Min.   :-186368    Min.   :    0       Min.   :  0.0       
##  1st Qu.:      0    1st Qu.: 1230       1st Qu.: 42.0       
##  Median :      0    Median : 2460       Median : 88.0       
##  Mean   :  43733    Mean   : 4861       Mean   :128.8       
##  3rd Qu.:  70512    3rd Qu.: 7935       3rd Qu.:169.0       
##  Max.   : 380120    Max.   :20146       Max.   :555.0       
##                     NA's   :15          NA's   :2           
##  Vaccinations_14day_average
##  Min.   :     0            
##  1st Qu.:     0            
##  Median :     0            
##  Mean   : 43467            
##  3rd Qu.: 73123            
##  Max.   :215604            
##  NA's   :2

From the above summary the minimum daily vaccinations has a negative value. 

data$Daily_Vaccinations = abs(data$Daily_Vaccinations)
summary(data)
##       Date             Total_Cases      Total_Recoveries   Total_Deaths   
##  Min.   :2020-03-02   Min.   :      0   Min.   :      0   Min.   :     0  
##  1st Qu.:2020-09-12   1st Qu.: 649004   1st Qu.: 577164   1st Qu.: 15437  
##  Median :2021-03-26   Median :1543079   Median :1469565   Median : 52602  
##  Mean   :2021-03-26   Mean   :1689264   Mean   :1571826   Mean   : 48749  
##  3rd Qu.:2021-10-06   3rd Qu.:2909262   3rd Qu.:2787270   3rd Qu.: 88043  
##  Max.   :2022-04-19   Max.   :3743582   Max.   :3628776   Max.   :100195

##  Median : 9752061   Median :       0   Median : 2261   Median : 82.0  
##  Mean   :10679451   Mean   : 8490692   Mean   : 4816   Mean   :128.6  
##  3rd Qu.:17881650   3rd Qu.:18623035   3rd Qu.: 7292   3rd Qu.:166.0  
##  Max.   :24212994   Max.   :34366442   Max.   :37875   Max.   :844.0  
##                                        NA's   :2                      
##  Daily_Vaccinations Cases_14day_average Deaths_14day_average
##  Min.   :     0     Min.   :    0       Min.   :  0.0       
##  1st Qu.:     0     1st Qu.: 1230       1st Qu.: 42.0       
##  Median :     0     Median : 2460       Median : 88.0       
##  Mean   : 44495     Mean   : 4861       Mean   :128.8       
##  3rd Qu.: 70982     3rd Qu.: 7935       3rd Qu.:169.0       
##  Max.   :380120     Max.   :20146       Max.   :555.0

##  1st Qu.:     0            
##  Median :     0            
##  Mean   : 43467            
##  3rd Qu.: 73123            
##  Max.   :215604            
##  NA's   :2

The variables Daily_cases,Cases_14day_average,Deaths_14day_average and Vaccinations_14day_average have missing values. The missing values will be replaced by the median of the variable 

data$Daily_Cases[which(is.na(data$Daily_Cases))] = median(data$Daily_Cases,na.rm = T)
data$Cases_14day_average[which(is.na(data$Cases_14day_average))] = median(data$Cases_14day_average,na.rm = T)
data$Deaths_14day_average[which(is.na(data$Deaths_14day_average))] = median(data$Deaths_14day_average,na.rm = T)
data$Vaccinations_14day_average[which(is.na(data$Vaccinations_14day_average))] = median(data$Vaccinations_14day_average,na.rm = T)

summary(data)
##       Date             Total_Cases      Total_Recoveries   Total_Deaths   
##  Min.   :2020-03-02   Min.   :      0   Min.   :      0   Min.   :     0  
##  1st Qu.:2020-09-12   1st Qu.: 649004   1st Qu.: 577164   1st Qu.: 15437

B. Univariate Analysis

  1. Daily Cases

Provincial Data 

summary(mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##     0.0    49.0   172.0   536.2   503.0 17716.0
par(mfrow=c(1,3))
hist(mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases, main = "Daily Cases",
     xlab = "Daily cases",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases), main = "Daily Cases distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Daily Cases")

summary(mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##     0.0    49.0   172.0   536.2   503.0 17716.0

The daily cases are skewed to the left as shown in the descriptive stats, histogram, density plot and boxplot. There appears to be outliers as shown by the boxplot but these values are extreme values which represent a time when COVID 19 cases were on the rise. The most daily cases observed in the data are between 0 and 1000. 

plot(mydata_sorted$Date,mydata_sorted$Daily_Cases,type = "l",lwd = 1, col = "#cc0000",main = "Daily COVID19 cases with time",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Daily COVID19 cases")

The first peak corresponds to the first COVID19 outbreak in May/June 2020. The second peak between end of 2020 and beginning of 2021 corresponds to the second outbreak. The peak in mid 2022 corresponds to the outbreak of the Delta variant, which has a higher transmissibility. Torwards the end of 2021 and beginning of 2022 there was an outbreak of the Omicron variant. This corresponds to the last peak in the plot above.

Daily cases for National dataset: 

summary(data$Daily_Cases)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0    1102    2261    4809    7262   37875
par(mfrow=c(1,3))
hist(data$Daily_Cases, main = "Daily Cases",
     xlab = "Daily cases",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(data$Daily_Cases), main = "Total Cases distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(data$Daily_Cases,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Daily Cases")

It is possible the maximum number of cases recorded in one day is 37875. Therefore the values above the 1.5IQR are possibly extreme values not errors. 

plot(data$Date,data$Daily_Cases,type = "l",lwd = 1, col = "#cc0000",main = "National Daily COVID19 cases with time",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Daily COVID19 cases")

ii Daily Deaths 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))
mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths = abs(mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths)
mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths = as.numeric(mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths)
hist(mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths, main = "Daily Deaths",
     xlab = "Daily deaths",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths), main = "Daily Deaths distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Daily Deaths")

The daily deaths are skewed to the left as shown in the descriptive stats, histogram, density plot and boxplot. There appears to be outliers as shown by the boxplot but these values are extreme values which represent a time when COVID 19 deaths were on the rise and this corresponds to the characteristics of the COVID19 Daily Cases. Most days recorded daily deaths between 0 and 50. The maximum number of deaths recorded a day is 515. 

summary(mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##    0.00    0.00    2.00   14.41   15.00  515.00
plot(mydata_sorted$Date,mydata_sorted$Daily_Deaths,type = "l",lwd = 1, col = "blue",main = "Daily COVID19 deaths with time",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Daily COVID19 deaths")

Daily deaths: National dataset 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))
hist(data$Daily_Deaths, main = "Daily Deaths",
     xlab = "Daily deaths",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(data$Daily_Deaths), main = "Daily Deaths distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(data$Daily_Deaths,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Daily Deaths")

plot(data$Date,data$Daily_Deaths,type = "l",lwd = 1, col = "blue",main = "Daily COVID19 deaths with time",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Daily COVID19 deaths")

iii Total Recoveries 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))
hist(mydata_sorted$Total_Recoveries, main = "Total Recoveries",
     xlab = "Total Recoveries",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(mydata_sorted$Total_Recoveries),main = "Total Recoveries", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(mydata_sorted$Total_Recoveries,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Total Recoveries")

iv Total Cases 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))
hist(mydata_sorted$Total_Cases, main = "Total Cases",
     xlab = "Total Cases",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(mydata_sorted$Total_Cases), main = "Total Cases distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(mydata_sorted$Total_Cases,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Total Cases")

v.Total Deaths 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))

hist(mydata_sorted$Total_Deaths, main = "Total Deaths",
     xlab = "Total deaths",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(mydata_sorted$Total_Deaths), main = "Total Deaths distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(mydata_sorted$Total_Deaths,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Total Deaths")

vi.Cases 14 day average 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))

hist(mydata_sorted$Cases_14day_average, main = "Cases:14day average",
     xlab = "cases:14day average",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(mydata_sorted$Cases_14day_average), main = "Cases:14day average", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(mydata_sorted$Cases_14day_average,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Cases 14day average")

vii.Deaths 14 day average 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))

hist(mydata_sorted$Deaths_14day_average, main = "Deaths:14day average",
     xlab = "Deaths 14day average",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(mydata_sorted$Deaths_14day_average), main = "Deaths 14day average distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(mydata_sorted$Deaths_14day_average,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Deaths 14day average")

  1. Daily Vaccinations 

par(mfrow=c(1,3))
data$Daily_Vaccinations = abs(data$Daily_Vaccinations)
hist(data$Daily_Vaccinations, main = "Deaths:14day average",
     xlab = "Deaths 14day average",
     ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "blue")
plot(density(data$Daily_Vaccinations), main = "Deaths 14day average distribution", col = "red",lwd = 2)
boxplot(data$Daily_Vaccinations,col = "blue",main="Boxplot:Deaths 14day average")

summary(data$Daily_Vaccinations)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0       0       0   44495   70982  380120
plot(data$Date,data$Daily_Vaccinations,type = "l",lwd = 1, col = "blue",main = "Daily COVID19 vaccinations with time",
     xlab = "Date",
     ylab = "Daily COVID19 vaccnations")

C.Multivariate Analysis 

by(mydata_sorted$Total_Cases,mydata_sorted$Province,summary)

by(mydata_sorted$Total_Deaths,mydata_sorted$Province,summary)
## mydata_sorted$Province: Eastern Cape

##       0    3045   11354    9006   14953   16605 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: Free State

##       0     817    3628    3669    6797    7671 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: Gauteng

##       0    3915   10213   10430   19429   20678 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: KwaZulu-Natal

##       0    2405   10152    8220   14644   16065 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: Limpopo
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0     258    2209    2042    4012    4586 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: Mpumalanga
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0     466    1338    1356    1972    4705 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: North West
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0     310    1455    1959    4156    4750 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: Northern Cape
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0     165     819    1134    2204    3219 
## ------------------------------------------------------------ 
## mydata_sorted$Province: Western Cape
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##       0    4056   11434   10933   19875   21916

Though Gauteng has had the highest number of total COVID19 cases, more people have died due to COVID19 related complications in Western Cape. 

boxplot(mydata_sorted$Total_Cases~mydata_sorted$Province,notch = T,col="blue",main =
          "Boxplot:Total Cases per province")

boxplot(mydata_sorted$Total_Deaths~mydata_sorted$Province,notch = T,col="#cc0000", main = 
          "Boxplots:Total Deaths per province")

The boxplots above give a relative comparison of the total cases and total deaths for all the provinces. 

plot(mydata_sorted$Total_Cases,mydata_sorted$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,
     col = "#cc0000",
     main = "Total cases vs Total deaths",
     xlab = "Total cases",
     ylab = "Total deaths")

There is a direct correlation between the total cases and total deaths.Different lines shown in the scatter plot represents 10 different provinces.

Scatter plots: Total cases vs Total deaths per province

1.Gauteng 

gauteng = subset(mydata_sorted,Province == "Gauteng")
plot(gauteng$Total_Cases,gauteng$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,
     col = "#cc0000",
     main = "Gauteng:Total cases vs Total deaths",
     xlab = "Total cases",
     ylab = "Total deaths")

We can define death rate as the number of deaths divided by the number of cases.In this case we took the maximum total of deaths divided by the maximum total cases. The death rate is 0.0170. This variable enables a comparison of the number of deaths as per number of cases to be compared province by province. This can probably give an indication of the quality of care for covid19 patients across provinces. For Gauteng the death rate is 0.017. Even though Gauteng recorded the highest total infections and is currently second highest in total deaths but the number of deaths per cases is lower than other provinces for instance Eastern Cape which is: 0.048.

  1. Eastern Cape 

EC = subset(mydata_sorted,Province == "Eastern Cape")
plot(EC$Total_Cases,EC$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,
     col = "#cc0000",
     main = "Eastern Cape:Total cases vs Total deaths",
     xlab = "Total cases",
     ylab = "Total deaths")

  1. Free State 

FS = subset(mydata_sorted,Province == "Free State")
plot(FS$Total_Cases,FS$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,
     col = "#cc0000",
     main = "Free State:Total cases vs Total deaths",
     xlab = "Total cases",
     ylab = "Total deaths")

Death rate = 0.0378

  1. KwaZulu-Natal 

kzn = subset(mydata_sorted,Province == "KwaZulu-Natal")
plot(kzn$Total_Cases,kzn$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,
     col = "#cc0000",

Death rate = 0.0243

Death rate = 0.0243

Death rate = 0.0294

plot(NW$Total_Cases,NW$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,

  1. Northern Cape

plot(NC$Total_Cases,NC$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,
     col = "#cc0000",
     main = "Northern Cape:Total cases vs Total deaths",
     xlab = "Total cases",

Death rate = 0.0295

  1. Western Cape 

WC = subset(mydata_sorted,Province == "Western Cape")
plot(WC$Total_Cases,WC$Total_Deaths,
     pch = 19,
     cex = 0.3,
     col = "#cc0000",
     main = "Western Cape:Total cases vs Total deaths",
     xlab = "Total cases",
     ylab = "Total deaths")

Death rate = 0.0336

D.Data Visualization using Tableau

(i)Total confirmed cases and deaths over time dashboard:

https://public.tableau.com/app/profile/mufaro.chinake/viz/SouthAfricaCOVID19ConfirmedCases/Dashboard1?publish=yes

(ii)Daily cases and deaths over time:

https://public.tableau.com/app/profile/mufaro.chinake/viz/SouthAfricaCOVID19DailyCasesandDeaths/Dashboard1?publish=yes
